methods.method_name: General Linear Models for Herbivore Functional Groups
methods.description: This method involves running general linear models (GLMs) to determine the probability of occurrence of herbivore functional groups. The models use herbivore biomass as the dependent variable and consider various environmental and risk factors as explanatory variables.

steps.description: Models included herbivore biomass as the dependent variable and probability of occurrence in relation to water, vegetation type, vegetation density, and proportion human risk (either low or high risk; both were not included in the same model).
steps.searchable_summary: Define GLM with herbivore biomass as dependent variable and environmental factors as explanatory variables

steps.description: We also included two-way interactions between all possible combinations of these variables.
steps.searchable_summary: Include two-way interactions between explanatory variables in GLM

steps.description: Categorical variables (vegetation type and vegetation risk) were included as factors in the GLMs.
steps.searchable_summary: Include categorical variables as factors in GLM

steps.description: Continuous variables (probability of occurrence in relation to water and herbivore biomass) were scaled between 1 and 0.
For the GLMs, we employed a model averaging approach using the best fitting models (those with a akaike information criterion [AICc] < 2) using the dredge function in the MuMIn package.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform model averaging using dredge function for best fitting models with AICc < 2

steps.description: All statistical analyses were carried out in the statistical software R.
